title: aspilia lisowskiana
intro: Aspilia lisowskiana, also known as African tea or Aspilia, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the leaves of the Aspilia lisowskiana plant are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antimalarial and antihelmintic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be consumed as a tea or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
